steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence introduces edge modeling as "one process" in general terms. The second sentence explains what that process specifically involves. The blank needs a transition word that signals a move from a general statement to a specific elaboration.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — adds new facts: signals addition of a separate point rather than a zoom-in on what was just introduced. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Specifically': marks the shift from a broad introduction to a precise explanation of that same thing. ✓
  • Choice C — draws a conclusion: implies the second sentence follows logically as a result, not as a clarification. ✗
  • Choice D — makes a comparison: suggests the second sentence contrasts with or parallels the first rather than detailing it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
